data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **Package references**: In xUnit v3, the separate packages `xunit.abstractions`, `xunit.extensibility.core`, and `xunit.extensibility.execution` are consolidated into `xunit.v3.extensibility`. The runner package `xunit.runner.visualstudio` is replaced by `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 or `xunit.v3.runner.visualstudio`. Let me check the correct package names for v3.

In xUnit v3:


- For extensibility: `xunit.v3.extensibility` replaces the three separate v2 packages
- For running tests: `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3.x handles the test runner integration

The namespace structure has changed too—`Xunit.Abstractions` is gone, and types like `ITestOutputHelper` are now directly in the `Xunit` namespace. Since this project needs extensibility, I should target `xunit.v3.extensibility` as the main package.

For the test runner, `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3.x is the right choice, though I should verify whether the `global.json` runner configuration needs updating since v3 can work with `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` as well as the traditional VSTest setup.

The main migration steps are straightforward: swap in the v3 packages, update the using statements since the types have moved into the `Xunit` namespace directly, and keep `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` for compatibility. I'm also wondering if `ITestOutputHelper` has been renamed or replaced in v3, which would affect how test output is handled.

Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.runner.visualstudio:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## About This Project

This project contains the VSTest adapter for xUnit.net. This adapter allows any VSTest-enabled test runner to run xUnit.net tests (which includes Test Explorer in Visual Studio 2022 and later). It supports .NET 8+ and .NET Framework 4.7.2+. It can run tests from xUnit.net 1.9.2 and later.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) (xUnit.net v3) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest) (all versions of xUnit.net).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

### Latest Builds

Latest stable

Latest CI ([how to use](https://xunit.net/docs/using-ci-builds))

Now I have a clear picture. Let me migrate the project:

1. Replace the 3 v2 extensibility packages with `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` (3.2.2)
2. Update `xunit.runner.visualstudio` to 3.1.5
3. Fix namespace changes in code

    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.core" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.execution" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />

    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="3.1.5" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the v3 packages. The project uses extensibility packages, so I need:

- `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` (3.2.2) - replaces `xunit.extensibility.core`, `xunit.extensibility.execution`, and `xunit.abstractions`
- `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (3.1.5) - updated runner

Let me also check what `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` depends on and what it provides.

Let me also check: this project is not a test project with tests, it's a project that extends xUnit (a custom discoverer/logger). 

Looking at the code more carefully, the `CustomDiscoverer.cs` file actually contains an `OutputLogger` class that uses `ITestOutputHelper`, so it's really a helper utility for tests. For the v3 migration, I need to replace those three v2 packages with `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` and update the runner to v3.1.5, then clean up the using statements.

In v3, `ITestOutputHelper` moved from `Xunit.Abstractions` to the `Xunit` namespace directly, though I should verify this works when building. I also need to consider whether `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` is still necessary depending on whether they're using VSTest or the newer Microsoft Testing Platform.

Looking at the project structure more carefully, this is an extensibility project with only extensibility packages. In v3, the package structure has consolidated—`xunit.abstractions`, `xunit.extensibility.core`, and `xunit.extensibility.execution` all merge into `xunit.v3.extensibility.core`. I'll need to remove the old `using Xunit.Abstractions;` statement and update the package references accordingly.

data.content:
Migration complete. Here's what changed:

**`TestProject.csproj`** — Replaced 3 v2 packages with 1 v3 package:
- `xunit.abstractions` (2.0.3) → removed (merged into v3 core)
- `xunit.extensibility.core` (2.9.3) → `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` (3.2.2)
- `xunit.extensibility.execution` (2.9.3) → `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` (3.2.2)
- `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2 → 3.1.5

**`CustomDiscoverer.cs`** — Removed `using Xunit.Abstractions;` since `ITestOutputHelper` now lives in the `Xunit` namespace in v3.
